behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP is the failure boundary: whether the draft is truly context-aware depends on real model output quality and variability. A mocked/model-stubbed test can pass while real generations become generic. MOCK-HIDE is high for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run the drafting step against a real LLM with varied real email threads containing concrete details (dates, asks, constraints). Assert the draft includes thread-specific references and fails a genericity heuristic across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS applies: contradiction detection usually depends on KB retrieval + claim extraction + checker wiring. Pure unit tests can miss failures in module interaction. Minimum is Integration unless the checker itself is an LLM judge, which would raise to Agentic.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real local KB and real contradiction cases (draft claims vs KB facts) through the full fact-check module stack (retrieval, matching, flagging). Verify contradictory claims are flagged and non-contradictions are not.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: If production contradiction detection is model-judged/non-deterministic, promote this behavior to Agentic.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily generation behavior under missing context. MOCK-HIDE is high: templated mocks won’t expose hallucination/fabrication risk seen with real model calls. LLM-DEP is direct.
behaviors.test_description: Run real LLM drafting with empty/irrelevant KB retrieval results and prompts that would tempt speculation. Assert the draft includes the required disclaimer and does not introduce unsupported factual claims across repeated runs.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state gating between approval state and send action. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of a single function can miss wiring bugs where send is triggered prematurely. No real external infra is strictly required for minimum confidence.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise the composed approval + send modules with a fake sender endpoint and user actions. Verify no send call occurs before explicit approval, and exactly one send occurs after approval.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: MULTI-STEP with agentic dependence: this validates a full pipeline containing at least one real LLM step plus downstream checks and approval gating. Single-step or isolated tests can miss cross-step failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run an end-to-end scenario using a real email thread fixture/source, real draft generation, real fact-check pass, and approval UI/state transition before send. Assert correct sequencing, artifacts at each stage, and final send only after approval.
